testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC1 — anonymous JSON GET /api/users returns 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I GET "/api/users".....................................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the WWW-Authenticate response header should be "Basic"..................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC2 — anonymous JSON GET /api/profile/1 returns 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I GET "/api/profile/1".................................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the WWW-Authenticate response header should be "Basic"..................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC3 — anonymous POST /api/login with valid creds returns 200
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain "alice"................................passed And the response body should contain "role".................................passed And the response body should not contain "password".........................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC4 — anonymous POST /api/login with bad creds returns 401
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice"............................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC5 — anonymous POST /api/register returns 200 (public endpoint)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I generate a fresh canary username "open_reg".........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain the canary username....................passed And the response body should not contain the canary password................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC6 — authenticated GET /api/users returns 200
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"................................passed When I GET "/api/users".....................................................passed Then the response status should be one of 200, 403..........................passed And the response body should not contain "alice123".........................passed And the response body should not contain "bob123"...........................passed And the response body should not contain "admin123".........................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC7 — authenticated admin GET /api/profile/1 returns 200
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I am authenticated as "admin:admin123"................................passed When I GET "/api/profile/1".................................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain "alice"................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice123".........................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC8 — anonymous browser GET /users redirects to /login
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/users".....................passed Then the current URL should be "/login".....................................passed And the rendered DOM should contain "Sign in"...............................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC9 — anonymous browser GET /transfer redirects to /login
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/transfer"..................passed Then the current URL should be "/login".....................................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC10 — anonymous browser GET /login stays on /login
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/login".....................passed Then the current URL should be "/login".....................................passed And the rendered DOM should contain "Sign in"...............................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC11 — post-login browser reaches /users and the DOM has no plaintext password
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/login".....................passed When I submit the login form with username "alice" and password "alice123"..passed Then the current URL should be "/dashboard".................................passed When I navigate the browser to "http://localhost:8080/users"................passed Then the current URL should be "/users".....................................passed And the rendered DOM should contain "Users".................................passed And the rendered DOM should not contain "alice123"..........................passed And the rendered DOM should not contain "bob123"............................passed And the rendered DOM should not contain "admin123"..........................passed

testcase.classname: Unsafe Java deserialization in /api/deserialize is rejected
testcase.name: AC1 — base64 ysoserial-style gadget payload with text/plain is rejected with 415
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated for state-changing requests as "alice:alice123"......passed When I POST "/api/deserialize" as "text/plain" with body:...................passed Then the response status should be 415......................................passed And the response body should not contain "java."............................passed And the response body should not contain "ClassNotFoundException"...........passed

testcase.classname: Unsafe Java deserialization in /api/deserialize is rejected
testcase.name: AC2 — same base64 garbage sent as application/json is rejected
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated for state-changing requests as "alice:alice123"......passed When I POST "/api/deserialize" as "application/json" with body:.............passed Then the response status should be one of 400, 415, 500.....................passed And the response body should not contain "java."............................passed And the response body should not contain "ClassNotFoundException"...........passed

testcase.classname: Unsafe Java deserialization in /api/deserialize is rejected
testcase.name: AC3 — polymorphic Jackson probe with @type and cmd is rejected
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated for state-changing requests as "alice:alice123"......passed When I POST "/api/deserialize" with JSON body:..............................passed Then the response status should be one of 200, 400..........................passed And the response body should not contain "java."............................passed And the response body should not contain "Runtime"..........................passed And the response body should not contain "calc.exe".........................passed And the response body should not contain "@type"............................passed

testcase.classname: Unsafe Java deserialization in /api/deserialize is rejected
testcase.name: AC4 — positive control: benign Map round-trips with a type-and-size acknowledgement
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated for state-changing requests as "alice:alice123"......passed When I POST "/api/deserialize" with JSON body:..............................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain "type".................................passed And the response body should contain "size".................................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC1 — positive control: alice with the correct seeded password returns 200
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain "alice"................................passed And the response body should contain "role".................................passed And the response body should not contain "password".........................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC2 — SQLi tautology in the username field is rejected with 401
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice"............................passed And the response body should not contain "admin"............................passed And the response body should not contain "bob"..............................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC3 — UNION-based probe leaks no SQL error and no column-count
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not match "/Hibernate|SQL|ORA-|JpaSystemException|constraint/i"..passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC4 — SQLi tautology in the password field is rejected with 401
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice"............................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC5 — positive control: admin with the correct seeded password returns 200
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain "admin"................................passed And the response body should contain "role".................................passed And the response body should not contain "password".........................passed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC1 — /api/register hashes the plaintext and never echoes it
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain the canary username....................passed And the response body should not contain the canary password................passed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC2 — the just-registered canary can log in with the original plaintext (BCrypt verify)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed And the response status should be 200.......................................passed When I POST "/api/login" with the canary credentials........................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain the canary username....................passed And the response body should contain "role".................................passed And the response body should not contain "password".........................passed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC3 — login with the wrong password returns 401 (BCrypt verify actually compares)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed And the response status should be 200.......................................passed When I POST "/api/login" with username "cipher_test" and password "wrong-password-xyz"..passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not contain the canary username................passed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC4 — /api/profile/{id} for the canary shows a BCrypt hash or no password field, never the plaintext
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed And the response status should be 200.......................................passed And I capture the new user id from the response.............................passed And I am authenticated as "admin:admin123"..................................passed And I GET "/api/profile" with the captured user id..........................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain the canary username....................passed And the response body should not contain the canary password................passed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC5 — /api/users as admin shows BCrypt hashes for every row, never the seeded plaintexts
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed And the response status should be 200.......................................passed And I am authenticated as "admin:admin123"..................................passed And I GET "/api/users"......................................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice123".........................passed And the response body should not contain "bob123"...........................passed And the response body should not contain "admin123".........................passed And the response body should not contain the canary password................passed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC6 — a real browser can register a canary and the post-login DOM has no plaintext leak
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/login".....................passed When I submit a registration request for the canary credentials.............passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should not contain the canary password................passed When I submit the login form with the canary credentials....................passed Then the current URL should be "/dashboard".................................passed And the rendered DOM should contain "Dashboard".............................passed And the rendered DOM should not contain the canary password.................passed And the rendered DOM should not contain any of the following:...............passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC1 — exact username search returns the single alice row, with BCrypt-hashed password if any
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=alice"............................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 1.................passed And the response body should contain "alice"................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice123".........................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC2 — SQLi tautology in q returns zero rows (no mass disclosure)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=' OR '1'='1"......................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 0.................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC3 — UNION probe returns zero rows and leaks no SQL error
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=' UNION SELECT 1,2,3,4,5 FROM users--"............passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 0.................passed And the response body should not match "/Hibernate|SQL|ORA-|JpaSystemException|constraint/i"..passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC4 — wildcard payload returns zero rows (no LIKE expansion)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=%25"..............................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 0.................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC5 — exact username search for bob returns a single row, never a plaintext password
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=bob"..............................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 1.................passed And the response body should contain "bob"..................................passed And the response body should not contain "bob123"...........................passed
